today_focus.title: OpenAI："We used to say build the MVP. Now you should build the system that builds the MVP first."
today_focus.brief: "We used to say build the MVP. Now you should build the system that builds the MVP first."。先放在观察位，等更多证据出现后再判断它的行业分量。
today_focus.social_original_text:
"We used to say build the MVP. Now you should build the system that builds the MVP first."

Here's my new episode with @ryancarson where he shared how he runs his startup solo with AI agents:

✅ OpenClaw as his AI chief of staff to triage emails, book meetings, and do sales outreach

✅ Codex and Devin as his AI eng team to ship features while he sleeps

Some quotes from Ryan:

"Spend a lot of time upfront setting up your skills + documentation. Then you've suddenly unlocked the work of 10 people."

"Treat your agent like a real employee. Give it a real email address, calendar access, and GitHub account."

"Pay a designer to set up your design system and brand. After that, you can use AI to generate on-brand assets."

📌 Watch now: https://t.co/Slc5cEkFGQ

Thanks to our sponsors:

@WisprFlow: Don't type, just speak https://t.co/oqHJ8bN3ll

@linear: The AI agent platform for modern teams https://t.co/yFylGfkenJ
today_focus.translation_zh: “我们过去常说‘先做 MVP’。现在你应该先构建一个能生成 MVP 的系统。” 这是我和 @ryancarson 的最新一期节目，他分享了如何独自借助 AI 智能体运营初创公司： ✅ 使用 OpenClaw 作为 AI 幕僚长，负责筛选邮件、安排会议和进行销售外联 ✅ 使用 Codex 和 Devin 作为 AI 研发团队，在他休息时也能持续交付功能 Ryan 的一些核心观点： “前期花大量时间配置好技能库与文档。之后你就能瞬间释放出相当于 10 个人的生产力。” “把你的智能体当成真正的员工。给它分配真实的邮箱、日历权限和 GitHub 账号。” “花钱请设计师搭建好设计系统与品牌规范。之后你就可以用 AI 生成符合品牌调性的素材。” 📌 立即观看：https://t.co/Slc5cEkFGQ 感谢赞助商： @WisprFlow：无需打字，直接语音 https://t.co/oqHJ8bN3ll @linear：面向现代团队的 AI 智能体平台 https://t.co/yFylGfkenJ
evidence_titles: "We used to say build the MVP. Now you should build the system that builds the MVP first."
evidence_summaries:
"We used to say build the MVP. Now you should build the system that builds the MVP first."

today_focus.title: CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis because they’re sufficiently distant from the last mile of work that still has to happen to generate most value with AI.
today_focus.brief: CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis because they’re sufficiently distant from the last mile of work that still has to happen to generate most value with AI。先放在观察位，等更多证据出现后再判断它的行业分量。
today_focus.social_original_text:
CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis because they’re sufficiently distant from the last mile of work that still has to happen to generate most value with AI.

So when they play with AI, they see the happy path results, often not considering the next 10 or 20 things that have to happen to get sustainable results from agents.

“Look I made this awesome product prototype”. Yes but you didn’t have to review the code before it went into production and fix a bunch of issues.

“Look I generated a contract”. Yes but you didn’t verify all the terms before it goes out to the counterparty and didn’t have to wire up all the past contracts to work with.

The best thing you can do as a CEO is to use AI a *ton* to figure out the real implications of agents in the enterprise, and come out the other side with an appreciation for both the upside and the real work that goes into them.
evidence_titles: CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis because they’re sufficiently distant from the last mile of work that still has to happen to generate most value with AI.
evidence_summaries:
CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis because they’re sufficiently distant from the last mile of work that still has to happen to generate most value with AI.

So when they play with AI, they see the happy path results, often not considering the next 10 or 20 things that have to happen to get sustainable results from agents.

“Look I made this awesome product prototype”. Yes but you didn’t have to review the code before it went into production and fix a bunch of issues.

“Look I generated a contract”. Yes but you didn’t verify all the terms before it goes out to the counterparty and didn’t have to wire up all the past contracts to work with.

The best thing you can do as a CEO is to use AI a *ton* to figure out the real implications of agents in the enterprise, and come out the other side with an appreciation for both the upside and the real work that goes into them.

worth_tracking.title: OpenAI：Is AGI already here?
worth_tracking.brief: Is AGI already here。先放在观察位，等更多证据出现后再判断它的行业分量。
worth_tracking.social_original_text:
Is AGI already here?

"I think if we froze the models that we have right now and you really worked on the harness and maybe we also spent more time training with a great harness, I think people would really feel the AGI in every single domain" - @yanndubs of @OpenAI https://t.co/amFrnChXkC https://t.co/nGv05BVzMI
worth_tracking.translation_zh: AGI 已经到来了吗？ “我认为，如果我们冻结现有模型，全力优化评估框架，并投入更多时间配合优质框架进行训练，人们将在各个领域真切感受到 AGI 的存在。”—— @OpenAI 的 @yanndubs https://t.co/amFrnChXkC https://t.co/nGv05BVzMI
evidence_titles: Is AGI already here?
evidence_summaries:

items.title: Introduction
items.title_zh: SkillOpt：智能体技能自进化执行策略
items.takeaway_zh: 提出SkillOpt框架，通过优化器将执行轨迹转化为技能增删改操作，并经门控验证后自动迭代智能体技能。
items.why_it_matters_zh: 突破仅靠权重或提示词微调的局限，实现智能体执行流程与领域技能的自动化持续进化。

items.title: 1 Images generated by Lens at 1440 resolution. Section provides more visualizations.
items.title_zh: Lens：重构文生图基础模型训练效率
items.takeaway_zh: 提出Lens方法，从模型规模、数据信息密度与收敛速度三维度优化文生图基础模型的训练效率。
items.why_it_matters_zh: 显著降低大模型训练的算力与资金成本，为高效训练高分辨率文生图模型提供新范式。

items.title: Rethinking Cross-Layer Information Routing in Diffusion Transformers
items.title_zh: 重新思考扩散Transformer中的跨层信息路由
items.takeaway_zh: 提出DAR模块替代传统残差连接，实现可学习、随时间步自适应的跨层信息聚合，提升扩散模型训练效率与生成质量。
items.why_it_matters_zh: 揭示扩散模型残差设计瓶颈，为DiT架构优化提供正交新方向，可无缝集成并加速大模型训练与微调。

items.title: SciAtlas: A Large-Scale Knowledge Graph for Automated Scientific Research
items.title_zh: SciAtlas：面向自动化科研的大规模知识图谱
items.takeaway_zh: 构建涵盖数千万论文的多学科异构学术知识图谱，结合神经符号检索算法，为AI科研智能体提供结构化认知底座。
items.why_it_matters_zh: 突破传统语义检索局限，以确定性关联发现降低智能体推理幻觉与成本，赋能自动化科研全流程。

items.title: StepAudio 2.5 Technical Report
items.takeaway_zh: 提出统一音频-语言基础模型，通过RLHF对齐与专用解码策略，在ASR、TTS和实时交互任务上匹敌或超越专用系统。
items.why_it_matters_zh: 证明单一基座可通过范式切换兼顾多项语音任务，为构建高效统一的语音大模型提供新路径。

items.title: OpenAI："We used to say build the MVP. Now you should build the system that builds the MVP first."
items.brief: "We used to say build the MVP. Now you should build the system that builds the MVP first."。先放在观察位，等更多证据出现后再判断它的行业分量。
items.social_original_text:
"We used to say build the MVP. Now you should build the system that builds the MVP first."

Here's my new episode with @ryancarson where he shared how he runs his startup solo with AI agents:

✅ OpenClaw as his AI chief of staff to triage emails, book meetings, and do sales outreach

✅ Codex and Devin as his AI eng team to ship features while he sleeps

Some quotes from Ryan:

"Spend a lot of time upfront setting up your skills + documentation. Then you've suddenly unlocked the work of 10 people."

"Treat your agent like a real employee. Give it a real email address, calendar access, and GitHub account."

"Pay a designer to set up your design system and brand. After that, you can use AI to generate on-brand assets."

📌 Watch now: https://t.co/Slc5cEkFGQ

Thanks to our sponsors:

@WisprFlow: Don't type, just speak https://t.co/oqHJ8bN3ll

@linear: The AI agent platform for modern teams https://t.co/yFylGfkenJ
items.translation_zh: “我们过去常说‘先做 MVP’。现在你应该先构建一个能生成 MVP 的系统。” 这是我和 @ryancarson 的最新一期节目，他分享了如何独自借助 AI 智能体运营初创公司： ✅ 使用 OpenClaw 作为 AI 幕僚长，负责筛选邮件、安排会议和进行销售外联 ✅ 使用 Codex 和 Devin 作为 AI 研发团队，在他休息时也能持续交付功能 Ryan 的一些核心观点： “前期花大量时间配置好技能库与文档。之后你就能瞬间释放出相当于 10 个人的生产力。” “把你的智能体当成真正的员工。给它分配真实的邮箱、日历权限和 GitHub 账号。” “花钱请设计师搭建好设计系统与品牌规范。之后你就可以用 AI 生成符合品牌调性的素材。” 📌 立即观看：https://t.co/Slc5cEkFGQ 感谢赞助商： @WisprFlow：无需打字，直接语音 https://t.co/oqHJ8bN3ll @linear：面向现代团队的 AI 智能体平台 https://t.co/yFylGfkenJ
evidence_titles: "We used to say build the MVP. Now you should build the system that builds the MVP first."
evidence_summaries:
"We used to say build the MVP. Now you should build the system that builds the MVP first."

Here's my new episode with @ryancarson where he shared how he runs his startup solo with AI agents:

✅ OpenClaw as his AI chief of staff to triage emails, book meetings, and do sales outreach

✅ Codex and Devin as his AI eng team to ship features while he sleeps

Some quotes from Ryan:

items.title: CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis because they’re sufficiently distant from the last mile of work that still has to happen to generate most value with AI.
items.brief: CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis because they’re sufficiently distant from the last mile of work that still has to happen to generate most value with AI。先放在观察位，等更多证据出现后再判断它的行业分量。
items.social_original_text:
CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis because they’re sufficiently distant from the last mile of work that still has to happen to generate most value with AI.

So when they play with AI, they see the happy path results, often not considering the next 10 or 20 things that have to happen to get sustainable results from agents.

“Look I made this awesome product prototype”. Yes but you didn’t have to review the code before it went into production and fix a bunch of issues.

“Look I generated a contract”. Yes but you didn’t verify all the terms before it goes out to the counterparty and didn’t have to wire up all the past contracts to work with.

The best thing you can do as a CEO is to use AI a *ton* to figure out the real implications of agents in the enterprise, and come out the other side with an appreciation for both the upside and the real work that goes into them.
evidence_titles: CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis because they’re sufficiently distant from the last mile of work that still has to happen to generate most value with AI.
evidence_summaries:
CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis because they’re sufficiently distant from the last mile of work that still has to happen to generate most value with AI.

So when they play with AI, they see the happy path results, often not considering the next 10 or 20 things that have to happen to get sustainable results from agents.

“Look I made this awesome product prototype”. Yes but you didn’t have to review the code before it went into production and fix a bunch of issues.

“Look I generated a contract”. Yes but you didn’t verify all the terms before it goes out to the counterparty and didn’t have to wire up all the past contracts to work with.

The best thing you can do as a CEO is to use AI a *ton* to figure out the real implications of agents in the enterprise, and come out the other side with an appreciation for both the upside and the real work that goes into them.

items.title: OpenAI：Is AGI already here?
items.brief: Is AGI already here。先放在观察位，等更多证据出现后再判断它的行业分量。
items.social_original_text:
Is AGI already here?

"I think if we froze the models that we have right now and you really worked on the harness and maybe we also spent more time training with a great harness, I think people would really feel the AGI in every single domain" - @yanndubs of @OpenAI https://t.co/amFrnChXkC https://t.co/nGv05BVzMI
items.translation_zh: AGI 已经到来了吗？ “我认为，如果我们冻结现有模型，全力优化评估框架，并投入更多时间配合优质框架进行训练，人们将在各个领域真切感受到 AGI 的存在。”—— @OpenAI 的 @yanndubs https://t.co/amFrnChXkC https://t.co/nGv05BVzMI
evidence_titles: Is AGI already here?
evidence_summaries:
Is AGI already here?

"I think if we froze the models that we have right now and you really worked on the harness and maybe we also spent more time training with a great harness, I think people would really feel the AGI in every single domain" - @yanndubs of @OpenAI https://t.co/amFrnChXkC https://t.co/nGv05BVzMI
